title: Brain Dissection Protocol
author: Ana Catarina Carapinha Cascalho
materials: Ensure all tools and materials are sterilized.
Wear appropriate personal protective equipment like a lab coat and gloves.
Lift the brain out of the skull gently.

steps.title: Set up a clean and organized workspace
steps.instruction: Set up a clean and organized workspace.

steps.title: Use cervical dislocation (no use of...
steps.instruction: Use cervical dislocation (no use of anesthesia to minimize anesthesia-related effects).

steps.title: With surgical scissors, remove the head...
steps.instruction: With surgical scissors, remove the head by cutting posterior from the ears.

steps.title: Make a midline incision in the skin using scissors
steps.instruction: Make a midline incision in the skin using scissors.

steps.title: Flip the skin over the eyes to expose the skull
steps.instruction: Flip the skin over the eyes to expose the skull.

steps.title: Use Iris scissors to make a...
steps.instruction: Use Iris scissors to make a small incision on the top of the skull, starting from the caudal part at the point of the (inter)parietal bone.

steps.title: Be cautious not to cut through the brain tissue
steps.instruction: Be cautious not to cut through the brain tissue.

steps.title: Make a firm cut through the...
steps.instruction: Make a firm cut through the most anterior part of the skull, between the eyes (frontal bone). This facilitates easier brain removal.

steps.title: Tilt one side of the parietal...
steps.instruction: Tilt one side of the parietal bone with the curved narrow pattern forceps and break it off.

steps.title: Repeat for the other side
steps.instruction: Repeat for the other side.

steps.title: If the frontal bone remains, make...
steps.instruction: If the frontal bone remains, make a small incision to facilitate its removal.

steps.title: Be careful not to damage the...
steps.instruction: Be careful not to damage the meninges between the brain and the skull.

steps.title: Once the brain is free from...
steps.instruction: Once the brain is free from meninges, slide the closed curved narrow pattern forceps under the anterior part of the brain (olfactory bulb).

steps.title: Gently tilt the forceps upward to lift the brain
steps.instruction: Gently tilt the forceps upward to lift the brain.

steps.title: Slide the forceps further down to...
steps.instruction: Slide the forceps further down to break optic nerves and other cranial nerves.

steps.title: Wash the brain thoroughly with ice-cold...
steps.instruction: Wash the brain thoroughly with ice-cold DPBS (Sigma; D8662) on the gelatin plate.

steps.title: Cut the brain into left and...
steps.instruction: Cut the brain into left and right hemisphere (OR DO THE NEXT STEP).

steps.title: Depending on the regions of interest,...
steps.instruction: Depending on the regions of interest, dissect the following areas: Cerebellum Cortex Interbrain Hindbrain

steps.title: Place dissected brain parts in labeled tubes
steps.instruction: Place dissected brain parts in labeled tubes.

steps.title: Snap freeze the tubes in liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: Snap freeze the tubes in liquid nitrogen. Always handle the tissues carefully to avoid damage. Work quickly to preserve tissue integrity. Store frozen brain parts at -80 °C until further analysis.
